title: Staff Software Development Engineer in Test, UI & API Agentic AI
company_focus: Cloud security platform and data protection
description:
About Netskope

Today, there's more data and users outside the enterprise
than inside, causing the network perimeter as we know it to
dissolve. We realized a new perimeter was needed, one that
is built in the cloud and follows and protects data wherever
it goes, so we started Netskope to redefine Cloud, Network
and Data Security.

Since 2012, we have built the market-leading cloud security
company and an award-winning culture powered by hundreds of
employees spread across offices in Santa Clara, St. Louis,
Bangalore, London, Paris, Melbourne, Taipei, and Tokyo. Our
core values are openness, honesty, and transparency, and we
purposely developed our open desk layouts and large meeting
spaces to support and promote partnerships, collaboration,
and teamwork. From catered lunches and office celebrations
to employee recognition events and social professional
groups such as the Awesome Women of Netskope (AWON), we
strive to keep work fun, supportive and interactive.  Visit
us at Netskope Careers. Please follow us on LinkedIn and
Twitter@Netskope.

About the role:

Please note, this team is hiring across all levels and
candidates are individually assessed and appropriately
leveled based upon their skills and experience.

We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Quality
Engineer to join our Agentic AI team. In this role, you will
be a custodian of quality for our cutting-edge agentic AI
platform & set of  security agents developed on that. You
will be instrumental in ensuring the reliability,
scalability, and security of a platform that handles complex
data across diverse cloud and on-premise environments.

This is not just a testing role; it's an opportunity to
influence the entire development lifecycle of Agentic AI.
We're looking for a proactive engineer who can identify gaps
and advocate for best practices, from unit and integration
testing within the development team to comprehensive end-to-
end validation. The ideal candidate is comfortable diving
deep into a complex architecture, creating robust test
strategies, and executing both manual and automated tests to
perfection.

What's in it for you:

Work at the forefront of Agentic AI, solving novel quality
challenges inherent in Agentic Systems and LLMs. This is a
chance to move beyond standard QA and define reliability and
safety standards for next-generation security products. You
will be instrumental in ensuring the reliability,
scalability, and security of a platform designed to handle
vast, complex data across diverse cloud and on-premise
environments.

What you will be doing:

- Design and execute comprehensive test plans for new
  features, covering everything from UI and APIs to backend
  processes and data integrations for the Agentic platform.
- Develop and maintain robust automation frameworks. You'll
  expand our UI testing with Playwright and build out our
  API and backend testing capabilities to ensure full
  coverage.
- Create a framework for Evaluation pipeline which other teams
  can use to plug and play the test cases for their agents
- Test across complex environments, including onboarding cloud
  accounts (AWS, Azure, GCP), interacting with various data
  stores and lakes, and validating on-premise integrations.
- Validate critical background jobs, such as long running
  agents, agentic workflows, data scans, classification, and
  alert generation, ensuring they are accurate and
  performant at scale.
- Own and evolve our regression and smoke testing suites to
  ensure release readiness and maintain product stability.
- Act as a quality champion, partnering with the development
  team to improve test coverage by identifying needs for
  better unit and integration tests.
- Develop strategies to manage testing across a wide variety
  of data store versions and types, ensuring our product
  remains compatible and reliable.
- Handle customer escalations, perform root cause analysis,
  and drive issues to resolution from a quality engineering
  perspective.
- Engage in cross-functional team standups to prioritize
  tasks, communicate progress, and collaborate to meet
  deadlines.

Required skills and experience:

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering,
  or a related field.
- 8+ years of professional experience in QA testing, with your
  most recent job in this area specifically
- Strong expertise in test automation. Proficiency with
  automation tools like Playwright, Selenium etc is
  required.
- Hands-on experience with testing long running workflows &
  pipelines on frameworks like Airflow, Kubeflow, Sagemaker
- Hands-on experience with major cloud providers (AWS, Azure,
  GCP) and a solid understanding of cloud architecture and
  services.
- Knowledge of Kubernetes and Docker, including testing
  applications deployed in containerized environments.
- Proven experience testing systems that interact with a
  diverse set of data stores (e.g., SQL, NoSQL, Data Lakes).
- Deep understanding of software testing methodologies,
  including smoke, regression, functional, and performance
  testing.
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines and tools (e.g., Jenkins,
  GitHub Actions, GitLab CI).
- Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to devise
  creative solutions for complex testing challenges, such as
  version compatibility.
- Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the
  ability to influence and partner effectively with
  development teams.

Education:

- BS CS or equivalent required, MS CS or equivalent strongly
  preferred

#LI-DS1

Netskope is committed to implementing equal employment
opportunities for all employees and applicants for
employment. Netskope does not discriminate in employment
opportunities or practices based on religion, race, color,
sex, marital or veteran statues, age, national origin,
ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition,
sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, genetic
information, pregnancy (including childbirth, lactation and
related medical conditions), or any other characteristic
protected by the laws or regulations of any jurisdiction in
which we operate.

Netskope respects your privacy and is committed to
protecting the personal information you share with us,
please refer to Netskope's Privacy Policy for more details.

The application window for this position is expected to
close within 50 days. You may apply by filling out the below
information, or visiting our Netskope Careers site.
responsibilities: Design and execute comprehensive test plans for new features, covering UI, APIs, backend processes, and data integrations for the Agentic platform
Develop and maintain robust automation frameworks using Playwright for UI testing and build API and backend testing capabilities
Create a framework for Evaluation pipeline which other teams can use to plug and play test cases for their agents
Validate critical background jobs including long running agents, agentic workflows, data scans, classification, and alert generation at scale
Own and evolve regression and smoke testing suites to ensure release readiness and maintain product stability
Handle customer escalations, perform root cause analysis, and drive issues to resolution from a quality engineering perspective
use_cases: Agentic AI platform testing and validation across cloud and on-premise environments
Security agents deployed on agentic AI platform for data protection
Cloud account onboarding and integration for AWS, Azure, and GCP
Long running agent workflows and data classification pipelines
Data store compatibility testing across SQL, NoSQL, and data lakes
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a Software Development Engineer in Test role focused
on testing and quality assurance for an agentic AI platform.
While working with AI systems, the role is about testing,
validation, and automation frameworks rather than building,
training, or deploying models and agents directly.
